_meta.description: Synthetic memory corpus for hindsight-vs-markdown benchmark. 50 atomic facts modeled on real project memory categories.

facts.title: User role: senior backend engineer
facts.content: User is a senior backend engineer with 8 years of Python experience and 2 years of Rust. Primary domain is distributed data pipelines.

facts.title: User prefers terse explanations
facts.content: User prefers terse explanations without preamble or trailing summaries. Skip pleasantries and get straight to the answer.

facts.title: User prefers vim keybindings
facts.content: User uses vim keybindings everywhere — IDE, shell (zsh vi-mode), browser (Vimium). Editor commands should assume vim muscle memory.

facts.title: User uses bun over npm
facts.content: User has standardized on bun for all JavaScript/TypeScript projects. Never suggest npm or yarn commands.

facts.title: User uses uv for Python
facts.content: User uses uv (not pip or poetry) for Python dependency management. uv pip install, uv venv, uv lock.

facts.title: Never mock the database in integration tests
facts.content: Integration tests must hit a real database, not mocks. Why: prior incident where mocked tests passed but the prod migration failed silently. How to apply: any test that exercises a query path uses a containerized Postgres.

facts.title: Never amend pushed commits
facts.content: Never use git commit --amend on a commit that has been pushed. Why: forces colleagues into recovery flows. How to apply: amend only local-only HEAD; otherwise create a fixup commit.

facts.title: Bundled refactor PRs are preferred
facts.content: For wide refactors in this codebase, user prefers ONE bundled PR over many small ones. Why: per-PR overhead exceeds the cognitive benefit when changes are mechanical. Validated 2026-03 after a successful 40-file rename PR.

facts.title: Profile before optimizing
facts.content: Never optimize without profiling first. Why: prior "obvious" hotspot fix turned out to be irrelevant — the real cost was DNS resolution. How to apply: py-spy or perf, then optimize.

facts.title: Avoid Helper/Util/Manager class names
facts.content: Don't name classes Helper, Util, Manager, or Service without a more specific qualifier. Why: those names accumulate unrelated methods and become god classes. How to apply: name by what the class IS, not what it does.

facts.title: Storage engine: SQLite for local cache
facts.content: Decided 2026-02 to use SQLite for the local cache layer instead of Postgres. Why: zero-config single-process workloads dominate; the operational simplicity outweighs the loss of concurrent writers. How to apply: any new persistent state at-rest goes to SQLite unless cross-process concurrent writers are required.

facts.title: Message broker: NATS over Kafka
facts.content: Chose NATS JetStream instead of Kafka for the event bus in 2026-Q1. Why: lower operational footprint, no Zookeeper dependency, sufficient throughput for our 10k msg/s ceiling.

facts.title: API style: gRPC for internal, REST for external
facts.content: Internal service-to-service communication uses gRPC. External API surface (third-party integrators) is REST + OpenAPI. Decided 2025-11 to standardize. No GraphQL anywhere.

facts.title: Q3 2026 deadline: SOC2 type 2 audit
facts.content: SOC2 Type 2 audit window opens 2026-07-01 and closes 2026-09-30. All access logging, encryption-at-rest, and incident response runbooks must be complete by 2026-06-15.

facts.title: Merge freeze before mobile release
facts.content: Merge freeze begins 2026-06-05 for the mobile team's release cut. Non-critical PRs scheduled after that date should be flagged. Freeze lifts 2026-06-12 after release branch tag.

facts.title: Auth rewrite driven by compliance
facts.content: The auth middleware rewrite is driven by legal/compliance requirements around session token storage, not tech-debt cleanup. Scope decisions favor compliance over ergonomics.

facts.title: Ana owns the ingest pipeline
facts.content: Ana is the primary maintainer of the ingest pipeline (services/ingest/). Route nontrivial questions about ingest behavior or schema decisions to her.

facts.title: Marco owns observability
facts.content: Marco owns the observability stack (Grafana, Loki, Tempo). Any change to log format, metric naming, or trace span structure goes through him.

facts.title: Defer ESM migration until 2026 Q4
facts.content: Deferred the CommonJS-to-ESM migration to 2026-Q4. Why: too many vendored dependencies still ship CJS-only; revisit when the long tail crosses 50% ESM.

facts.title: Incident 2026-03-12: rate limiter cascade
facts.content: Incident 2026-03-12: a misconfigured rate limiter on the auth endpoint cascaded into a 47-minute partial outage. Root cause: missing per-tenant bucket; one noisy tenant exhausted the global pool. Fix: per-tenant token buckets shipped 2026-03-15.

facts.title: Latency dashboard for oncall
facts.content: The Grafana board at grafana.internal/d/api-latency is the oncall watch dashboard. Touching request handling means watching this for at least 30 minutes after deploy. Alert threshold: p99 > 800ms for 5 minutes.

facts.title: Pipeline bugs in Linear INGEST
facts.content: Pipeline-related bugs are tracked in the Linear project named INGEST. Backend bugs unrelated to pipelines go to BE.

facts.title: Architecture decision records location
facts.content: All architecture decision records live in docs/decisions/ as ADR-NNNN-title.md. Index in docs/decisions/INDEX.md. New decisions need ADR before code.

facts.title: Database failover runbook
facts.content: The database failover runbook is at runbooks/db-failover.md. Tested quarterly; last tested 2026-02-08. Estimated RTO: 4 minutes.

facts.title: Status page is hosted on statuspage.io
facts.content: Public status page at status.example.com is hosted via statuspage.io. Component updates require login at manage.statuspage.io with the shared 1Password entry "statuspage-admin".

facts.title: CI pipeline lives in .github/workflows
facts.content: All CI runs through GitHub Actions, configured under .github/workflows/. Required check is named "ci/main". Branch protection requires green ci/main + 1 approval.

facts.title: Secrets stored in HashiCorp Vault
facts.content: Secrets live in HashiCorp Vault at vault.internal:8200. Path convention: secret/data/{env}/{service}/{key}. Local development uses .envrc + direnv, never committed.

facts.title: Always smoke-test staging before prod
facts.content: Every prod deployment must be preceded by a successful staging smoke test (the script at scripts/smoke.sh). Why: caught a broken migration path in 2026-01 that would have hit prod otherwise.

facts.title: Feature flags via GrowthBook
facts.content: All new features ship behind GrowthBook flags. Flag naming: feature.{team}.{name}. Rollout default: 0%, then 10/50/100 over 3 days. Flag cleanup obligation: remove within 30 days of 100% rollout.

facts.title: On-call rotation is weekly
facts.content: On-call rotation is weekly, Monday 09:00 to next Monday 09:00 in Europe/Rome time. Handoff requires a written summary in #oncall-handoff. Page response SLA: 5 minutes.

facts.title: Avoid abbreviations in identifiers
facts.content: Don't abbreviate identifiers. user_count not usr_cnt, transaction not txn (except in well-established domain context like SQL transactions). Reading speed beats typing speed.

facts.title: Never swallow exceptions silently
facts.content: Never use bare except: pass or catch (e) {}. Either handle the error meaningfully, log it with context, or let it propagate. Why: silent swallowing has cost us debugging hours multiple times.

facts.title: Cuts stay cut — no resurrected code
facts.content: When code is removed, it stays removed. Don't restore deleted modules "just in case" without a current consumer. Why: dead code rot is harder to fix than re-deriving needed code from git history.

facts.title: Backend language: Python 3.12
facts.content: Backend services use Python 3.12. Pinned in pyproject.toml. No 3.11 syntax that breaks 3.12, no 3.13 features that aren't backported. Mypy strict mode.

facts.title: Frontend stack: React 19 + Vite + Tailwind
facts.content: Frontend uses React 19 with Vite as the bundler and Tailwind CSS v4 for styling. State management: Zustand (no Redux). Routing: TanStack Router.

facts.title: Database: Postgres 16
facts.content: Postgres 16 is the primary database. Migrations managed via Alembic for Python services, sqlx-cli for Rust services. No ORM beyond SQLAlchemy Core (no ORM relationships).

facts.title: Container runtime: Podman in dev, K8s in prod
facts.content: Local development uses Podman (Docker Desktop banned for licensing reasons). Production runs on Kubernetes 1.30 in EKS.

facts.title: User in Europe/Rome timezone
facts.content: User is in Europe/Rome (CET/CEST). Standard working hours 09:00-18:00 local. Async-friendly outside those windows.

facts.title: No marketing language in docs
facts.content: Documentation must avoid marketing language. Use "untested", "MVP", "needs validation" instead of "production-ready", "battle-tested", "enterprise-grade". Why: false confidence misleads readers and rots trust.

facts.title: Customer support tool is Zendesk
facts.content: Customer support tickets live in Zendesk at support.example.com. SDK-related tickets get auto-tagged "sdk" and routed to the platform team.

facts.title: Cache layer: Redis Cluster
facts.content: Redis Cluster (6 nodes, 3 primaries / 3 replicas) for the hot cache. TTL convention: 5 minutes for read-through caches, 1 hour for computed aggregates. No long-lived keys without an explicit eviction story.

facts.title: Never log raw secrets or PII
facts.content: Never log raw secret values, API keys, tokens, or PII fields (email, phone, address). Use structured logging with explicit field redaction. Repeat offense triggers a postmortem.

facts.title: Code review SLA: 24h business hours
facts.content: Code review SLA is 24 business hours from PR-ready (CI green, description complete). After 24h the author may merge with one self-approval if the change is non-architectural.

facts.title: User prefers Sonnet for code review
facts.content: For code review tasks the user prefers Claude Sonnet over Opus. Why: faster turnaround, sufficient quality for review-style tasks. Reserve Opus for architecture and debugging.

facts.title: Team chat is Slack workspace example.slack.com
facts.content: Team chat: Slack workspace example.slack.com. Channels: #eng-general (default), #incidents (oncall), #releases (deployment notifications), #oncall-handoff (weekly handoffs).

facts.title: Reject Helm in favor of Kustomize
facts.content: K8s manifests use Kustomize, not Helm. Decided 2025-09 after Helm's templating proved hard to debug at scale. Base + overlays per environment in deploy/k8s/.

facts.title: No 'You are absolutely right'
facts.content: Never start a reply with "You are absolutely right" or similar sycophantic openers. Wastes tokens, performative, undermines actual agreement when warranted.

facts.title: Linting: ruff for Python, biome for JS/TS
facts.content: Lint stack: ruff for all Python (replaces flake8/isort/black). Biome for JavaScript and TypeScript (replaces ESLint and Prettier). Pre-commit enforced; CI re-runs.

facts.title: Build only what was asked
facts.content: Build only what's asked. No speculative features, no hypothetical-future abstractions. MVP first; iterate based on real use, not imagined needs.

facts.title: Cloud spend tracked in Vantage
facts.content: AWS and GCP spend tracked in Vantage at app.vantage.sh. Monthly review meeting first Tuesday of each month. Cost allocation tags required: team, service, environment.
